Title: A Viral Satellite RNA Induces Yellow Symptoms on Tobacco by Targeting a Gene Involved in Chlorophyll Biosynthesis using the RNA Silencing Machinery

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Cucumber mosaic virus Y satellite RNA
Common_Genes.Applied_Species: Tobacco; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis; Tomato; Pepper
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana tabacum; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum; Capsicum annuum
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic pigment required for green coloration and photosynthesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Accumulates in infected systemic leaves; Y-sat-derived siRNAs accumulate abundantly, especially from SYR-containing hotspot regions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induces bright yellow mosaic/vein yellowing by generating siRNAs that target host ChlI mRNA and impair chlorophyll biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic inverted-repeat expression, small RNA Northern blot, small RNA deep sequencing, host-specific mutagenesis of SYR, 5'-RACE of ChlI cleavage, GFP-YR sensor assay
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for Y-sat IR transgene
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Requires CMV helper virus for infection in most assays; dsRNA transfection into protoplasts also used
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AGO1; host RNA silencing machinery; possible RDR involvement (proposed model)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Small RNA deep sequencing on Illumina Genome Analyzer (no accession reported); microarray and proteomics used to identify downstream effects (no accession reported)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Causes chlorosis/yellowing through host gene silencing; mutations can revert in planta (noted for Y-sat mut-Tom)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful model for designing artificial small RNAs or avoiding off-target host silencing by viral/satellite sequences; not a breeding target per se
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Y-sat produces abundant 21- and 22-nt siRNAs, including many from the satellite yellow region (SYR). 2. These siRNAs specifically target the complementary 22-nt region in host ChlI mRNA. 3. Altering SYR to match tomato or Arabidopsis ChlI redirects yellow symptom induction to those hosts. 4. Y-sat is sufficient to trigger ChlI silencing-associated yellowing through RNA silencing machinery.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Non-coding pathogenic determinant rather than a plant gene/protein, but it is a core experimental causal factor in the paper. Species specificity is dictated by 22-nt complementarity between SYR and host ChlI yellow region.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Tobacco; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis; Tomato; Pepper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana tabacum; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum; Capsicum annuum
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Magnesium chelatase subunit I; AAA+ ATPase-type chlorophyll biosynthesis enzyme subunit
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the ATP-dependent insertion of Mg2+ into protoporphyrin IX as part of magnesium chelatase, the first committed step of chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for direct biosynthesis of chlorophyll precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and chloroplast pigment formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme; first step in chlorophyll synthesis/chlorophyll branch as stated
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf tissues; assessed in systemic infected leaves, transgenic leaves, and protoplasts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation of ChlI causes yellow mosaic/vein yellowing and impairs chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic Y-sat inverted-repeat expression; qRT-PCR; Northern blot; CMV-based VIGS of ChlI; 5'-RACE cleavage mapping; GFP sensor assay; host-range/mutational complementation with Y-sat variants
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for Y-sat IR transgene; agroinfiltrated GFP sensor vector promoter not explicitly specified
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CMV infection with or without Y-sat; Y-sat dsRNA transfection; growth room 16-h light/8-h dark at 24°C
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray (no accession reported); small RNA deep sequencing on Illumina Genome Analyzer (no accession reported); proteomics LC-MS/MS (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Yellowing reflects impaired chlorophyll biosynthesis and chloroplast dysfunction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily a mechanistic pathogenicity study; suggests ChlI should be protected from sequence-specific RNA silencing to avoid chlorophyll loss rather than targeted for enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChlI mRNA contains a 22-nt region complementary to the Y-sat yellow-inducing sequence. 2. ChlI transcript is strongly downregulated in Y-sat-infected plants, Y-sat IR transgenic plants, and Y-sat dsRNA-treated protoplasts. 3. CMV vector-mediated silencing of ChlI alone reproduces the yellow symptom. 4. 5'-RACE mapped cleavage to the expected center of the complementary region, and GFP sensor assays confirmed sequence-specific targeting. 5. Engineering Y-sat to match tomato or Arabidopsis ChlI conferred yellowing in those hosts, supporting causality.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper refers to ChlI as 'the key gene involved in chlorophyll synthesis'. Arabidopsis ChlI1 but not ChlI2 supported yellowing when matched by mutant Y-sat, consistent with lower functionality/expression of ChlI2.
